title: Cantinoa pinetorum
intro: Cantinoa pinetorum, also known as Pinaleaf or Pine Cantinoa, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its various health benefits, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Cantinoa pinetorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cantinoa pinetorum include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Cantinoa pinetorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to South America, and its classification is part of the broader Lamiaceae family. Within this family, Cantinoa pinetorum is further grouped into the genus Cantinoa, which was previously considered part of the genus Hyptis. The exact classification of Cantinoa pinetorum may be subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical classification. Its classification reflects the ongoing evolution of plant classification systems.
